content.problemStatement:
NNN balls are placed at **distinct** coordinates on the X−X-X−axis. The initial coordinate of the ithi^{th}ith ball is given as Ai (Ai>0)A_i \ (A_i \gt 0)Ai​ (Ai​>0).

A hole is present at the origin. The ithi^{th}ith ball moves towards the hole with a speed of BiB_iBi​ units per second.

If multiple balls collide **before** reaching the hole, they merge into one ball, taking the maximum speed of the initial balls.

Determine the number of balls that will reach the hole considering all possible collisions along the way.

Note that collisions do not occur at the hole. Thus, if multiple balls reach the hole at the same time, all are counted separately.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of balls.
The second line contains NNN **distinct** positive integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the initial coordinates of balls.
The third line contains NNN integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ — the speed with which the ithi^{th}ith ball is moving towards the hole.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer denoting the number of balls that reach the hole.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The first ball reaches the hole in 111 second, and the second ball reaches in 222 seconds. Since they do not collide at any point, both balls will reach the hole separately.
Test case 222: The second and third balls collide midway. As a result, only two balls will reach the hole.
Test case 333: The first and second balls collide midway. After this, no further collisions occur. Thus, three balls reach the hole.
